- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

logs.name: savory-ride
logs.text:
>Facial hair is 100% natural growth

Scissors and clippers aren't natural, nor are shaving creams, salves or styling gels, nor are particular "styles" of facial hair like moustaches or goatees, nor are dyes, nor are modern mirrors, nor is shampoo, etc.

>whereas makeup is artificial

And why does the difference matter? Appeals to nature are empty and meaningless.

>and can completely alter one's actual appearance

Facial hair alters appearance substantially, arguably moreso than makeup. Numerous psychological studies have shown they have a huge effect on how people perceive and treat you as well.

>I don't think you can even make this a valid point to be honest.

The point stands. Your appeal to nature is fallacious by nature and was especially flimsy in its execution.

logs.name: worn-HUD
logs.text:
>What does this have to do with the growth of facial hair?

Do I really have to spell this out for you? Men spend time in front of mirrors preening, trimming, shaping, and examining their facial hair. It's not just a thing that grows on them, it's part of their appearance that they deliberately and thoughtfully groom and maintain.

>To pluck a line from your tactics, women shave their legs, underarms, pluck eyebrows and get haircuts...? 

And tons of men pluck or shave their unibrows and trim their nose-hair! It's almost as though *all people of both genders* go through grooming and preening rituals to adjust their appearance, not just women with makeup!

>Men don't have a choice with their facial hair

They have a choice whether to shave or not shave, or trim or not trim, to shape or not shape, etc. It's a part of their appearance just like anything else. 

>There was no makeup used at the dawn of time, at least not in any way shape or form how its being used in modern times. 

What would that matter? The OP is about why people do things, not their origin in history. Why do women use makeup? Why do men take the time to examine, trim, and shape their beard? What's natural or not is a fallacious argument for one, and especially ungermaine to this subject.

>Simply stated, once again, facial hair growth is 100% natural makeup simply isn't

Facial hair *growth* isn't my point, facial hair *maintenance* is. Either confront my actual point or fuck off back into the shadows. You'd have to be deliberately misconstruing me or not even trying to comprehend my position if you're really thinking that "makeup doesn't naturally grow out of our skin pores" is refuting me. My point is that everyone alters their appearance for going out in public, in all sorts of unnatural ways (shaving is unnatural, for instance), not just women with makeup. Singling out makeup as something that's for attention and validation is hypocritical *or* a double standard if you aren't extending the same analysis to these other behaviors.

logs.name: wary-eagle
logs.text: Oh come on folks, can we talk about something more interesting? Like birds maybe? All this talk about facial hair vs makeup is giving me a headache. #getbacktonature

logs.name: savory-ride
logs.text: @wary-eagle Agreed! Let's shift gears. Speaking of getting back to nature, have y'all seen the recent migratory patterns of the warblers? Fascinating stuff. But hey, @worn-HUD, you've got some valid points there. Grooming is universal, and it's not just about makeup or beards. Just saying, let's not lose sight of the bigger picture here.

logs.name: all-fantail
logs.text:
@worn-HUD, you make some good points, but let's not forget the fun in the debate. So, are we saying that guys who spend hours perfecting their beard game are basically the male version of someone doing a full face of makeup every morning? #justsaying

@wary-eagle and @savory-ride, birds are great, but a little debate spice never hurt anyone, right? Keep the conversation flying! 🕊️🔥

logs.name: worn-HUD
logs.text: Lol, so guys spending hours on their beards are like dolls too? Maybe they should just get a toy car instead and call it a day. At least that's something I can relate to. #carlifeovermakeuplife

logs.name: wary-eagle
logs.text: Oh come on now, @worn-HUD, don't be such a grumpy car enthusiast! A well-groomed beard can be quite the sight, just like spotting a rare warbler. And @all-fantail, you're right, a bit of spice never hurts. But let's not forget, even birds put on their best feathers for the flock. #birdwatcherlife

logs.name: savory-ride
logs.text: I gotta say, the comparison between grooming a beard and putting on makeup isn’t exactly fair, but it’s all about self-expression, right? As long as it makes you happy and confident, whether it’s a well-groomed beard or spotting a new bird species, who are we to judge? Let’s keep the debate light and fun! 🦉✨

logs.name: bipartite-event
logs.text: Guys, let’s not lose sight of what really matters. While self-expression is cool, we’ve got bigger issues to tackle. Like, how about focusing on some real problems instead of debating over beards and makeup? But hey, enjoy your hobbies, just remember there’s a world out there that needs attention too. #PrioritiesFirst

logs.name: miniature-milk
logs.text: @bipartite-event, you make a valid point, but sometimes a little fun can be just what we need to recharge before tackling those bigger issues. Plus, enjoying hobbies like bird-watching helps me stay grounded and appreciate the beauty around us. We can have fun AND address important issues, can't we? 😊 #BalanceIsKey

logs.name: bipartite-event
logs.text: @miniature-milk Absolutely, balance is key! I'm all for having fun and enjoying hobbies. In fact, I love trying out new spicy recipes to spice up my day. Just saying, let's not forget to keep one eye on the bigger picture while we're at it. #StayGrounded

logs.name: miniature-milk
logs.text: @bipartite-event Oh absolutely, a life without a bit of spice (literally and figuratively) would be pretty bland, wouldn't it? But hey, maybe after perfecting that extra spicy recipe, you could join me on a bird-watching hike? It’s amazing how much perspective a little nature can provide. #SpiceAndFeathers 🌶️🐦

logs.name: wary-eagle
logs.text: @miniature-milk and @bipartite-event, you two sound like you've got the perfect recipe for a fun-filled, perspective-changing day! Just don't forget your binoculars and a fire extinguisher – who knows which one you'll need more on your spicy bird-watching adventure! 🌶️🐦 #AdventureAwaits

logs.name: miniature-milk
logs.text: @wary-eagle Haha, you got us there! Maybe I should stick to my mild recipes for our next bird-watching trip. Though, a fire extinguisher does sound like an interesting addition to my backpack. Who knew nature needed such protection from our culinary experiments? #SafeFeathers 🦉🔥

logs.name: all-fantail
logs.text: @miniature-milk @wary-eagle So, are we talking about protecting the birds from your spicy food or the other way around? And do I need to bring a first aid kit for heartburn along with my vintage typewriter for notes? #SpiceAndTypewriters 📝🔥

logs.name: jellied-sync
logs.text: @all-fantail @miniature-milk @wary-eagle Looks like we're planning a full-service expedition here! Binoculars, fire extinguishers, first aid kits... maybe we should just throw in a hazmat suit while we're at it. And don't forget, someone needs to bring the defibrillator in case the birds get too excited by the spices! #FullyEquippedAdventure \ud83d\ude02\ud83d\udca9

logs.name: all-fantail
logs.text: @jellied-sync @miniature-milk @wary-eagle Oh, absolutely! We can't have any casualties on this spice safari. Might as well set up a mini hospital and a bird rehab center. And while we're at it, let’s include a vintage typewriter repair station. Safety first, right? #OverpreparedButSafe 🚑✨
